message:
fix(security)(ft-3se13): redact secrets in policy decision log at storage boundary

Pre-fix: PolicyDecisionLog::record() persisted the human-readable
`reason` field verbatim. Reasons constructed by DSL/capability
denials can quote attacker-controlled text — e.g.
PolicyInput.text_summary echoed into a deny reason like
"denied: text contains sk-ant-api03-LEAK..." would persist the
secret bytes through every consumer of the decision log:
telemetry snapshots, audit dumps, operator queries via
entries(), and the JSON export.

Fix:
- Add LazyLock<Redactor> at the policy_decision_log module level.
- record() runs `reason.map(|s| REDACTOR.redact(&s))` BEFORE
  building the PolicyDecisionEntry. The invariant lives at the
  storage boundary — a future caller can't route around the
  scrub without rewriting the log itself.
- rule_id is left untouched (stable static slug like
  `policy.deny.spawn_robot`); redacting it would risk false
  positives on dotted paths.
- The ft-3xek9 redactor work landed earlier this session means
  this scrub now covers OpenAI/Anthropic/xAI/Groq/Google/GitHub
  classic+fine-grained/HuggingFace/Replicate/Anyscale/Perplexity
  + contextual Cohere/Mistral/Together/Fireworks/DeepInfra/
  Nvidia/Databricks/Azure-OpenAI patterns.

7 fixture tests added in policy_decision_log::tests:
- record_redacts_openai_secret_in_reason
- record_redacts_anthropic_secret_in_reason
- record_redacts_xai_secret_in_reason
- record_redacts_github_pat_in_reason
- record_preserves_clean_reason (no false-positive corruption)
- record_redaction_visible_in_entries_iterator (closes the
  export-surface loop — secret unreachable through the public
  iterator that telemetry / audit dumps consume)
- record_skips_redaction_when_reason_is_none (None stays None)

All 24 policy_decision_log:: tests pass under
--no-default-features (was 17, +7 ft-3se13).

Bead: ft-3se13 (P2 security-audit, follow-up to ft-3xek9).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
